title: Pericarp RNA Extraction

steps: Determine the amount of plant material. Do not use more than 100mg. The tissue should have been flash frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80º.

Place the frozen tissue in a cold 2mL, round-bottom eppendorf tube with 2-3 stainless steel balls. Using a homogenizer-shaker, grind the tissue with the stainless steel balls for ~45 seconds at 30 Hz. Frozen tissues should not be allowed to thaw during handling.
Add 450μl of prepared lysis buffer to a maximum of 100mg tissue powder. Vortex vigorously. A short 1–3 min incubation at 56°C may help to disrupt the tissue. undetermined, Prepared lysis buffer
Transfer the lysate to a QIAshredder spin column (lilac) placed in a 2 ml collection tube, and centrifuge for 2 min at full speed. 00:02:00, spin at full speed
Mix the flow-through and ethanol together by pipetting gently up and down until the solution looks homogenous.

Transfer the sample (usually 650μl), including any precipitate that may have formed, to an RNeasy spin column (pink) placed in a 2mL collection tube and centrifuge briefly. Discard the flow-through. 00:00:25, Hold
Add 350μl Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column and centrifuge to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through. undetermined, Buffer RW1 00:00:25, Hold
Add 500μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column and cetrifuge to wash the membrane. Discard the flow-through. undetermined, Buffer RPE 00:00:25, Hold
Add 500μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy spin column and centrifuge to wash the membrane. undetermined, Buffer RPE

After centrifugation, carefully remove the RNeasy spin column from the collection tube so that the column does not contact the flow-through. Place the RNeasy spin column in a new 2 mL collection tube, and centrifuge with the cap open to dry the membrane. Discard the old collection tube with the flow-through. 00:02:00, Centrifuge at >
Place the RNeasy spin column in a labeled 1.5mL collection tube. Add 50μl RNase-free water directly to the spin column membrane and centrifuge to elute the RNA. undetermined, RNase-free water
Examine the ground tissue for visible clumps, and, if necessary, re-cool them in liquid nitrogen and repeat the grinding. go to step #8
Make up a lysis buffer with the following compenents. Vortex to mix. undetermined, Buffer RLC undetermined, 2-mercaptoethanol undetermined, PVP

Arrange tubes an dcolumns in order of use: QIAshredder (lilac), absolute ethanol tube, RNeasy spin column (pink), empty collection tube, final collection/storage tube. Label the final collection/storage tube with the sample name, date, and your initials. All other tubes can be given a shorthand label/number. Final Label: 1 DPA Pericarp RNA AR 19 Mar 18

Make up a batch ofRNase-free 80% ethanol. You will need 500μL for each RNA extraction sample. This can be made in a single large batch for all samples. undetermined, Absolute ethanol undetermined, RNase-free water

Make up a batch of DNAse solution for each RNA sample and keep on ice. undetermined, Resuspended DNase undetermined, Buffer RDD These components are from the RNase-free DNase kit. The resuspended DNase is made up from lyophilized powder and stored frozen in aliquots of ~50µL. Once thawed, the resuspended DNase cannot be refrozen, but may be used within 6 months if kept refrigerated. Buffer RDD is also kept refrigerated.

Carefully transfer the flow-through to the eppendorf tube with 0.5 volumes of absolute ethanol without disturbing the cell-debris pellet in the collection tube. You may discard the QIAshredder column and collection tube.
Add 80μL of the prepared DNase solution directly to the membrane and incubate at room temperature. undetermined, Prepared DNAse solution 00:15:00, Incubate at room temperature
Add 350μl Buffer RW1 to the RNeasy spin column and centrifuge to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flow-through. undetermined, Buffer RW1 00:00:25, Hold
Ensure that ethanol is added to Buffer RPE before use.

Prepare one eppendorf with 0.5 volumes of absolute ethanol for each RNA extraction sample. If you are using 450μL of prepared lysis buffer, this would be 225μL of absolute ethanol. Label the tube and close the lid to prevent evaporation.
Add 500μL of 80% ethanol to the RNeasy spin column and centrifuge to wash the membrane. Discard the flow-through. undetermined, 80% ethanol
Measure the concentration of the sample along with the 260/280 and 260/230 ratios. Record the concentration on the side of the tube. Store the sample at -80ºC.
creator: Alex Rajewski
